claim: Focus modes that constrain action space improve both agent output quality and user expectation alignment; mode-specific behaviors prevent confusion
actionable: Add mode selection to agent UIs (planning, research, debug, etc.); constrain available tools and system prompts per mode

claim: Transparent execution with visible tool calls, reasoning, and task progress list builds trust and enables early user intervention before agent compounds mistakes
actionable: Surface tool call inputs/outputs, task progress, and reasoning chains; allow user interruption at each step

claim: Personalization optimizes for speed to understanding (how the user would approach it) rather than speed to output; saves iteration cycles
actionable: Build user profiles (playbooks, memories, principles) into agent context; prioritize understanding user intent over fast generic responses

claim: Reversibility at multiple granularity levels (line, file, conversation state) bounds downside risk and encourages users to delegate higher-value tasks
actionable: Implement undo at multiple levels: line-level edits, file-level accepts/rejects, conversation-level rollback

claim: Parallel model execution from same input (without committing to one) enables experimentation and selection of best output
actionable: Support running multiple models or prompts in parallel; let users compare outputs and select the best
